---
title: Bookmark Button
sidebar: components
appPreview:
  file: components/inputs/bookmark-button/app-preview.py
  static: true
listing:
- id: example
  template: ../../_partials/components-detail-example.ejs
  template-params:
    dir: components/inputs/bookmark-button/
  contents:
  - title: Preview
    file: app-detail-preview.py
    height: 300
  - title: Express
    file: app-express.py
    shinylive: https://shinylive.io/py/editor/#code=NobwRAdghgtgpmAXAAjFADugdOgnmAGlQGMB7CAFzkqVQDMAnUmZAZwAsBLCXLOAD3QM4rVsk4x0pBhXER0AVwpFhEACZwGRBZwA6EfTqzdFFAPpV+FABS7IsOHaJ2AmqQUNk0eIifI7dgCUhpzG8kpmAEakpADWMFAMsVFKFOTWgcgAxMgAPLn6+gACqhoMWJYU+hp0yADmwnAU3HUZvhDIncjCFB4ddHYAEnAANiOkRCAmSljecBnI0sgA5KwUDFAQdZrLAL4AhAEQhKgUuOgIKGCVYLsAukA
  - title: Core
    file: app-core.py
    shinylive: https://shinylive.io/py/editor/#
- id: relevant-functions
  template: ../../_partials/components-detail-relevant-functions.ejs
  contents:
  - title: ui.input_bookmark_button
    href: https://shiny.posit.co/py/api/ui.input_bookmark_button.html
    signature: ui.input_bookmark_button(id='bookmark_button', label='Bookmark', icon=None,
      title='Bookmark the current application state', **kwargs)
---

:::{#example}
:::

:::{#relevant-functions}
:::

## Details

A bookmark button captures the current state of your app's inputs and saves it to the URL. Users can share this URL or save it to return to the exact same app state later.

**Note**: Bookmarking requires enabling it when creating your app. This is an advanced feature that requires additional configuration.

To add a bookmark button to your app:

  1. Add `ui.input_bookmark_button()` to the UI. You can customize the button's `label` and `icon`.

  2. When clicked, the button captures all input values and encodes them in the URL query parameters.

  3. When someone visits the bookmarked URL, the app automatically restores all inputs to their saved values.

**Enabling Bookmarking**: To use bookmark buttons, you must enable bookmarking when creating your app. The exact method depends on how you're running your app, but typically involves passing an `enableBookmarking` parameter.

**What Gets Bookmarked**: By default, all input values are bookmarked. This includes:
  - Text inputs
  - Numeric inputs
  - Sliders
  - Select/dropdown choices
  - Checkbox and radio button selections
  - Any other input component

**Use Cases**:
  - **Sharing Analysis**: Users can share specific parameter combinations
  - **Saving Progress**: Save complex configurations for later
  - **Documentation**: Create links to specific app states in documentation
  - **Testing**: Easily test specific input combinations

**URL Persistence**: The bookmark is stored in the URL query string, making it:
  - Easy to share via copy/paste
  - Compatible with browser bookmarks
  - Preservable in documentation or emails

**Advanced**: For more control over what gets bookmarked, you can use `@reactive.effect` with `onRestore` functions to customize the bookmark/restore behavior.

See Also: For more details on bookmarking, see the [Bookmarking](https://shiny.posit.co/py/docs/workflow.html#bookmarking) section in the Shiny documentation.
